Block.Para: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (assaults against public transit operators)

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Criminal Code to require a court to consider the fact that the victim of an assault is a public transit operator to be an aggravating circumstance for the purposes of sentencing.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Criminal Code (assaults against a public transit operators)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (assaults against public transit operators)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 269:

Block.MarginalNote: Aggravating circumstance ГўВЂВ” assault against a public transit operator
Block.Para: 269.01 (1) When a court imposes a sentence for an offence referred to in paragraph 264.1(1)(a) or any of sections 266 to 269, it shall consider as an aggravating circumstance the fact that the victim of the offence was, at the time of the commission of the offence, a public transit operator engaged in the performance of his or her duty.
(2) The following definitions apply in this section.

Block.MarginalNote:
ГўВЂВњpublic transit operatorГўВЂВќ
Г‚В« conducteur de vГѓВ©hicule de transport en commun Г‚В»
Block.Para: ГўВЂВњpublic transit operatorГўВЂВќ means an individual who operates a vehicle used in the provision of passenger transportation services to the public, and includes an individual who operates a school bus.
ГўВЂВњvehicleГўВЂВќ includes a bus, paratransit vehicle, licensed taxi cab, train, subway, tram and ferry.
Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
